title: Remedies for Employment Discrimination — EEOC
description: EEOC page summarizing relief available when employment discrimination is found: equitable remedies (placement, reinstatement, back pay, front pay), injunctive relief, compensatory damages (out-of-pocket + emotional harm), punitive damages (malicious/reckless intentional discrimination), attorney's fees, and liquidated damages for intentional ADEA/EPA violations — including per-employer-size statutory caps.
notes: Combined compensatory + punitive caps by employer size: 15–100 employees = $50,000; 101–200 = $100,000; 201–500 = $200,000; 500+ = $300,000. ADEA/EPA intentional violations yield liquidated damages (equal to back pay) instead of compensatory/punitive.
